install.note: OpenScience is an open-source, model-agnostic AI research workbench (open-source Claude Science alternative). It runs a local server and opens a workspace UI in the browser. Install via npm (preferred, cross-platform), the curl install script (macOS/Linux), or a platform binary from GitHub Releases. EchoBird configures the model by writing `~/.config/openscience/openscience.json` — a `provider.echobird` block (apiKey + baseURL; npm = @ai-sdk/anthropic for the Anthropic protocol, @ai-sdk/openai-compatible otherwise) plus `model`/`small_model` selectors pointing at `echobird/<model_id>`. OpenScience reads config per request, so a switch takes effect with no restart. Login, onboarding, and the Credentials panel stay OpenScience's own — its first-run onboarding auto-skips when launched without a TTY.

install_flow.summary: Install the `openscience` binary onto PATH (npm global, curl script, or GitHub release archive), then launch with `openscience serve` to start the local backend.
agent_steps: Detect OS and architecture. OpenScience ships native binaries for macOS (arm64/x64), Linux (x64/arm64), and Windows (x64).

Preferred path (all platforms): if npm is available, run `npm install -g @synsci/openscience` — this puts `openscience` on PATH and is the simplest cross-platform install. Verify with `openscience --version`.

macOS / Linux without npm: run `curl -fsSL https://openscience.sh/install | bash`. The script detects OS/arch, downloads the platform archive, extracts to ~/.openscience/bin, and modifies shell rc files (.zshrc / .bashrc) to add that dir to PATH. Tell the user to open a NEW terminal afterwards so PATH reloads.

Windows without npm: download https://github.com/synthetic-sciences/OpenScience/releases/latest/download/openscience-windows-x64.zip, extract openscience.exe to %USERPROFILE%\.openscience\bin, and add that directory to the user PATH. Verify with `openscience --version` in a new terminal.

After install, tell the user to launch with `openscience serve` in a terminal — it starts the local backend and prints the workspace URL (e.g. `openscience server listening on http://localhost:xxxxx`). The user opens that URL in their browser themselves. `serve` is headless and does NOT auto-open a browser; bare `openscience` (the `web` command) WOULD auto-open one, so use `serve` when launched from EchoBird.

OpenScience reads `~/.config/openscience/openscience.json` per request (a model switch takes effect with no restart). When the user picks a model in EchoBird, EchoBird writes an `echobird` provider block + active-model selectors to that file, so the chosen model becomes active in the workspace. The user can alternatively power models with a provider key env var (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, etc.), `openscience keys add`, or the free demo / Atlas managed models — those routes are independent of the EchoBird-written block.
tell_user: Which install method was used (npm global, curl script, or manual binary)
That `openscience` is now on PATH (may need a NEW terminal to take effect)
How to launch: `openscience serve` in a terminal (headless — prints the workspace URL, does not auto-open a browser)
To power models: pick a model in EchoBird (it writes the config to openscience.json), or set a provider key env var / run `openscience keys add` directly in OpenScience
